title: Fresh Juicy Chinese Hake Wonton Soup
description: A sour and juicy Chinese dish featuring hake, served as a wonton soup, perfect for a weeknight dinner
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and blanch the hake pieces for 30 seconds.
Immediately transfer the hake to an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, sesame oil, grated ginger, and chopped green onions.
Lay a wonton wrapper on a flat surface and place a small piece of the hake in the center of the wrapper.
Brush the edges of the wrapper with a small amount of water and fold the wrapper into a triangle, pressing the edges together to seal the wonton.
Repeat the process with the remaining hake and wonton wrappers.
In a large cast-iron skillet, combine chicken broth, rice vinegar, salt, and white pepper, and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and add the wontons to the skillet, cooking for 5-7 minutes or until they float to the surface.
Serve the wontons in the hot broth, garnished with additional green onions if desired.
